transcript: all rightight  recording a new note for this sample  i found this one resting loose on the surface ash layer just about  meters south of the main crip crater rim  so it looks like it might be a piece of old  the main matrix is a very dark  almost charcoal gray basalt  and it's highly vsicular with lots of tiny gas bubbles trapped throughout the ground mass  what's really interesting though is when you look at the fresh broken face  you can clearly see these small brownish green glassy fenchrsts  i'm highly confident  these are pigeonite crystals  bing pigeonite here is significant because it indicates the magma was erupted at an exceptionally high temperature  and then quenched very rapidly on the surface before the crystal structure could the exterior has a slight reddish brown oxidation rnd from weathering  it feels pretty dense despite the vesicles about the size of a large grape fruit  i'll bag this one up for further petrographic analysis to confirm the cooling rate
